taxonomy: Asarca chrysochlora belongs to the phylum Rotifera and is classified as a species of rotifer, a type of small, microscopic freshwater animal. The Rotifera phylum is part of the larger group called Lophotrochozoa, which also includes other invertebrates like mollusks and annelids. Asarca chrysochlora is further classified within the family Asarridae. This classification places it within the larger group of rotifers, which are characterized by their distinctive cilia and wheel-like movement. Asarca chrysochlora's classification is an important tool for understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.
